Mountain Road — Systems Specification Document
notes:
MOUNTAIN ROAD PROJECT — SYSTEMS SPECIFICATION DOCUMENT

1. ACCESS CONTROL SYSTEM
Ubiquiti UniFi facial recognition readers will be installed at five primary entry points: beach gate, vehicle gate, front door, downstairs bathroom, and upstairs lounge. Each reader integrates with Intercom functionality, allowing staff and owners inside the house to answer intercom calls from external visitors and manage guest access remotely. Guest access management supports three methods: temporary PIN codes, RFID tags, and facial photo recognition. All access control devices connect to the central UniFi network and management interface, enabling real-time monitoring and access logging through both the web dashboard and mobile app.

2. NETWORKING INFRASTRUCTURE
Ubiquiti UniFi Wi-Fi 7 access points will be distributed throughout all main living spaces with cabling and devices concealed through joinery. All access points home-run to a central network switch at the head end. A separate dedicated Wi-Fi 7 network will be provisioned for the tree house office to ensure independent bandwidth for work activities. The entire networking infrastructure operates on a unified UniFi backbone, enabling seamless roaming, integrated management through UniFi Controller, and integration with the Crestron control system and mobile apps.

3. CCTV AND PERIMETER SECURITY
Ubiquiti UniFi AI-powered cameras will be deployed across the property with integrated spotlights that automatically illuminate zones when motion or threats are detected.
